pairs.question: What is Jay Vidovich's current coaching position and which team does he coach?
pairs.answer: Jay Vidovich is the head coach of the University of Pittsburgh men's soccer team.

pairs.question: What were Jay Vidovich's most successful seasons as the head coach of Wake Forest University's soccer team?
pairs.answer: His most successful seasons were 2007 and 2008. In 2007, Wake Forest won the NCAA D1 Championship with a 22–2–1 record and were ranked #1 for most of 2008, though they fell in the Final Four to North Carolina.

pairs.question: How many ACC regular season championships did Jay Vidovich win during his tenure at Wake Forest?
pairs.answer: Jay Vidovich won three ACC regular season championships during his time as head coach at Wake Forest.

pairs.question: What was Jay Vidovich's coaching record at Wake Forest from 1994 to 2014?
pairs.answer: Jay Vidovich had a coaching record of 225 wins, 87 losses, and 31 ties during his 20-year tenure at Wake Forest from 1994 to 2014.

pairs.question: Which professional soccer team did Jay Vidovich coach before joining the University of Pittsburgh?
pairs.answer: Jay Vidovich was the head coach of Portland Timbers 2 in USL Pro before joining the University of Pittsburgh.

pairs.question: In which years was Jay Vidovich named the NCAA Men's Division 1 Coach of the Year?
pairs.answer: Jay Vidovich was named the NCAA Men's Division 1 Coach of the Year in both 2007 and 2008.

pairs.question: What notable achievements did Jay Vidovich have as an assistant coach at Wake Forest from 1986 to 1993?
pairs.answer: Jay Vidovich served as an assistant coach at Wake Forest from 1986 to 1993, contributing to the team's success before becoming the head coach in 1994.
